summary: Bug Fix Verified: 'I'm having a moment' error resolved in useChatSubmit hook. The wrapper function signature issue where routeIntent was called with (query, petId, miraMode) instead of ({ userId, petId, query, pet, token, userCity }) has been fixed by passing original functions directly from useChat.js.

login_flow.details: Login with dipali@clubconcierge.in / test123 redirects to /dashboard successfully

mira_demo_page_load.details: Page loads without crashing on tablet viewport (768x1024)

chat_message_send.details: Message 'I need a dog walker for next week' sent successfully

mira_response_no_error.details: Mira responded with actual helpful content about dog walking services - NO 'I'm having a moment' error

response_relevance.details: Response mentions: Lola (pet name), Maltese (breed), dog walking, timing preferences, Concierge service - highly relevant

service_flow.details: Quick replies generated (Morning walks, Afternoon walks, Flexible timing), Pet Concierge card displayed with WhatsApp/Chat/Email options

bug_verification.bug_description: 'I'm having a moment' error when sending chat messages due to incorrect wrapper function signatures in useChatSubmit hook
bug_verification.root_cause: Wrapper functions were calling routeIntent with wrong signature: (query, petId, miraMode) instead of ({ userId, petId, query, pet, token, userCity })
bug_verification.fix_applied: Removed wrapper functions at lines 2140-2148 in MiraDemoPage.jsx, now passing original functions directly from useChat.js
bug_verification.fix_verified: YES - Chat messages now work correctly without errors

console_logs_analysis.context_tracking: [INTELLIGENCE] Tracking 3 items for pronoun resolution - Working
console_logs_analysis.quick_replies: [QUICK REPLIES] Generated 3 suggestions for pillar: care - Working
console_logs_analysis.voice_status: [MIRA VOICE] Voice not triggered - voiceEnabled: false - As expected

context_for_next_testing_agent: Bug fix for 'I'm having a moment' error has been verified. The useChatSubmit hook now correctly passes original functions from useChat.js without wrapper functions. Chat functionality is working correctly.
